---
title: "Chebyshev Approximation Polynomial"
bibliography: ../../../references.bib
---

← [Numerical Methods](../)

Source inspiration: [@mathewsSite].

## Description

Chebyshev-node interpolation uses clustered nodes near interval endpoints to reduce oscillation compared with equally spaced interpolation at the same degree. This is why Chebyshev-based polynomial approximation is a standard remedy for Runge-type endpoint blow-up.

On an interval $[a,b]$, $n$ Chebyshev nodes are the mapped roots

$$
x_k = \frac{a+b}{2} + \frac{b-a}{2}\cos\!\left(\frac{(2k-1)\pi}{2n}\right),\qquad k=1,\ldots,n.
$$

## Animations

Each animation increases polynomial degree one step at a time for a fixed function and approximation interval.

[Julia source for all cases](chebyshev_animations_all.jl)

### Case 01

![Case 01 caption: f(x)=1/(1-x), interval [-1.6, 0.9].](cheby01.gif)

### Case 02

![Case 02 caption: f(x)=1/(1+x), interval [-0.9, 1.6].](cheby02.gif)

### Case 03

![Case 03 caption: f(x)=1/(1-x)^2, interval [-1.6, 0.9].](cheby03.gif)

### Case 04

![Case 04 caption: f(x)=sqrt(x), interval [0, 4] (expanded).](cheby04.gif)

### Case 05

![Case 05 caption: f(x)=sqrt(x), interval [0, 8].](cheby05.gif)

### Case 06

![Case 06 caption: f(x)=sqrt(x), interval [0, 10].](cheby06.gif)

### Case 07

![Case 07 caption: f(x)=1/sqrt(1-x), interval [-1.6, 0.9].](cheby07.gif)

### Case 08

![Case 08 caption: f(x)=1/(1+10x^2), interval [-1, 1], up to order 31.](cheby08.gif)

### Case 09

![Case 09 caption: f(x)=log(x), interval [0.02, 2].](cheby09.gif)

### Case 10

![Case 10 caption: f(x)=log(x), interval [0.02, 4].](cheby10.gif)

### Case 11

![Case 11 caption: f(x)=log(1+x), interval [-0.98, 1].](cheby11.gif)

### Case 12

![Case 12 caption: f(x)=sin(x), interval [-3pi, 3pi].](cheby12.gif)

### Case 13

![Case 13 caption: f(x)=cos(x), interval [-3pi, 3pi].](cheby13.gif)

### Case 14

![Case 14 caption: f(x)=tan(x), approximation interval [-1.45, 1.45] inside singularities.](cheby14.gif)

### Case 15

![Case 15 caption: f(x)=exp(x), interval [-2, 3].](cheby15.gif)

### Case 16

![Case 16 caption: f(x)=cos(x)*exp(-x), interval [-2, 4].](cheby16.gif)

### Case 17

![Case 17 caption: f(x)=cosh(x), interval [-4, 4].](cheby17.gif)

### Case 18

![Case 18 caption: f(x)=atan(x), interval [-2.2, 2.2].](cheby18.gif)

### Case 19

![Case 19 caption: f(x)=asin(x), interval [-1.5, 1.5].](cheby19.gif)

### Case 20

![Case 20 caption: f(x)=besselj(0,x), interval [-10.2, 10.2].](cheby20.gif)

### Case 21

![Case 21 caption: f(x)=besselj(1,x), interval [-10.2, 10.2].](cheby21.gif)

### Case 22

![Case 22 caption: f(x)=normal_pdf(x), interval [-3, 3].](cheby22.gif)

### Case 23

![Case 23 caption: f(x)=normal_cdf(x), interval [-3, 3].](cheby23.gif)

### Case 24

![Case 24 caption: f(x)=gamma(x), interval [0.05, 2].](cheby24.gif)

### Case 25

![Case 25 caption: f(x)=gamma(x), interval [0.05, 4].](cheby25.gif)

### Case 26

![Case 26 caption: f(x)=gamma(x), interval [0.5, 5].](cheby26.gif)

### Case 27

![Case 27 caption: f(x)=bessely(0,x), interval [0.5, 20].](cheby27.gif)

### Case 28

![Case 28 caption: f(x)=bessely(0,x), interval [0.5, 10].](cheby28.gif)

### Case 29

![Case 29 caption: f(x)=bessely(0,x), interval [0.5, 6] (expanded).](cheby29.gif)
